requirement: Supplier onboarding process is designed for engaging suppliers with whom the organization intends to conduct business with or procure goods and services.
Supplier data accuracy, completeness, and consistency of supplier master data across Coupa and SAP must be ensured.
Unvetted suppliers or services must go through the proper approval channels.
Information for all suppliers must be accurate and current.
Visibility to certifications and contact information must be ensured.
Multiple channels of managing information (e.g. paper forms, emails, phone calls, spreadsheets) must be eliminated.
Supplier participation must be encouraged.
Coupa SIM must manage the Supplier creation and onboarding process.
A Supplier will only be created in Coupa once they have completed the onboarding process.
Data accuracy, completeness, and consistency of supplier master data across Coupa and SAP must be ensured.
Supplier data must be accurate and current.
Supplier information must be accurate and current.

Internal supplier registration in Coupa is used when a supplier is onboarded by internal users due to urgency, business constraints, or specific supplier types, while still adhering to governance and approval controls.
The One-Time Vendor (OTV) process is designed for suppliers engaged for a single or non-recurring transaction.
The OTV process retains the existing structure while streamlining approvals and reducing data capture and documentation requirements.
Coupa uses different status labels to help users quickly see the status of each supplier.
The Supplier Portal Status column is used to see CSP statuses in the Suppliers table.
Vendors are responsible for ensuring that all required documentation is accurate, complete, and up to date to facilitate timely review and approval.
The list of documents that are required to be completed or attached by vendors during the onboarding and update processes is detailed in the Excel sheet referenced below.
The respective geography’s subject matter experts will need to provide us with the necessary translation for custom information added in the system.
Coupa supports the auto-translation of the standard information on the user interface for the following languages.
Buyer can verify supplier information and update it in the system.
System supports supplier self-service for updating their information.
System sends notifications to suppliers for updates and changes.
Buyer can create purchase requisitions for approved suppliers.
System supports approval workflow for purchase requisitions.
System generates purchase orders automatically from approved purchase requisitions.
System tracks and reports on ESG metrics for suppliers.
System supports compliance with local language requirements.
System generates reports on compliance status for suppliers.
